bids.reasoning: At t=0.00 there is no time pressure to concede, so I am targeting my maximum-utility bid by holding firm on the best value for every issue. The opponent’s opening offer gives me very low utility and provides no evidence of concessions yet, so I should counter with a strong anchor using only valid domain values.

At t=0.02 it is still very early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid rather than making large concessions. The opponent has only moved slightly from all valueF to issueA:valueE while keeping the rest at valueF, which gives me very low utility and does not yet justify major concessions; I will make a small non-repeating concession on issueC while holding my strongest positions.

At t=0.04 it is still very early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making only a small concession from my ideal package. The opponent has conceded only on issueA so far while keeping issueB/C/D at valueF, but issueA is my most important issue, so I will hold firm there and vary a less costly issue instead.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.84 because it is still very early at t=0.06 and there is no need to make major concessions yet. The opponent has only conceded on issueA while keeping issueB, issueC, and issueD at low-value options for me, so I will hold firm on my most important issues and make only a small variation to avoid repeating my previous bid exactly.

At t=0.08 it is still very early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making only a modest concession from my ideal. The opponent has mostly conceded on issueA, which is important to me, but their offers remain low on issueB/issueC/issueD, so I will hold firm on my strongest values while slightly varying my bid to avoid repeating my previous exact offer.

At t=0.10 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a modest concession to keep progress going. The opponent has conceded substantially on issueA up to my best value, but is still holding low-value positions for me on issueB/issueC/issueD, so I will hold firm on most issues and concede only slightly on issueB.

I am targeting a high-utility early-round bid, since time is only 0.12 and there is no need to make large concessions yet. The opponent has conceded substantially on issueA and somewhat on issueB, but is still holding issueC and issueD at valueF, so I will keep my most important issueA and issueD at valueA while offering a modest concession on issueC.

I targeted a high but slightly more accommodating bid around 0.80 utility, since it is still early at t=0.14 and there is no need to concede heavily yet. The opponent has clearly conceded on issueA and partially on issueB, while holding issueC and issueD at valueF, so I am matching their latest issueB movement to valueD while holding firm on my important preferred values for issueA, issueC, and issueD.

I am targeting a still-high utility bid around the early-game range, since at t=0.16 there is no need to make large concessions yet. The opponent’s offers are clearly improving for me on issueA and issueB, while they keep issueC and issueD at valueF, so I concede modestly on issueC but hold firm on the most important issueA and on issueD for now.

At t=0.18 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a small concession to avoid deadlock. The opponent has conceded substantially on issueA and issueB but remains firm on issueC and issueD at valueF, so I will hold my high-weight issueA and issueD while softening issueC slightly from my prior position.

At t=0.20 it is still early, so I am targeting a relatively strong utility around 0.72 rather than accepting the opponent’s latest low offer. The opponent has conceded on issueA and issueB but is holding firm on issueC and issueD, so I will keep my highest-priority issueA and issueD strong while making a new concession on issueC to test movement without over-conceding.

I am targeting a still-strong utility while making a meaningful early concession at t=0.22, since there is no need to drop close to the opponent’s current bid yet. The opponent has conceded on issueA and issueB but is holding firm on issueC=valueF and issueD=valueF, so I will concede issueC fully while protecting issueD, which is still valuable to me.

I am targeting a moderate utility above the opponent’s latest offer while conceding further on issueD, since the opponent has consistently accepted my preferred issueA and moved to issueB valueC but is holding firm on issueC valueF and low issueD values. At t=0.24 there is still time, so I should not drop all the way to their last bid, but I can make a meaningful compromise by keeping issueC at their apparent target and moving issueD closer to them without accepting their worst option for me.

I targeted a modest concession around utility 0.536, since we are still early-mid at t=0.26 and should not drop all the way to the opponent's current offer yet. The opponent has conceded substantially on issueA and issueB but is holding firm on issueC=valueF and now pushing issueD toward valueE/F, so I keep my highest-weight issueA and acceptable issueB while conceding on issueC and issueD. This avoids repeating my last bid and tests whether giving them issueD=valueF can unlock agreement without accepting their lower-utility issueD=valueE.
